print.description: Receives any number of arguments and prints their values to stdout, converting each with tostring. Values are tab-separated with a trailing newline.

type.description: Returns the type of its only argument, coded as a string: "nil", "number", "string", "boolean", "table", "function", "thread", or "userdata".

tostring.description: Converts any value to a string. If the metatable has a __tostring metamethod, calls it.

tonumber.description: Converts its argument to a number. An optional base (2–36) interprets the string in that base. Returns nil if conversion fails.

pairs.description: Returns the next function, the table t, and nil, enabling iteration over all key–value pairs with a generic for.

ipairs.description: Returns an iterator for integer keys 1, 2, … until a nil value is found. Suitable for arrays/sequences.

next.description: Returns the next index of the table and its associated value. When called with nil as the second argument, returns an initial index and value.

select.description: If index is a number, returns all arguments after that position. If index is "#", returns the total argument count.

rawget.description: Gets table[index] without invoking the __index metamethod.

rawset.description: Sets table[index] = value without invoking the __newindex metamethod.

rawequal.description: Checks whether v1 equals v2 without invoking the __eq metamethod.

rawlen.description: Returns the length of a table or string without invoking the __len metamethod. Available in Lua 5.2+.

setmetatable.description: Sets the metatable for the given table. If metatable is nil, removes the metatable. If the original metatable has a __metatable field, raises an error.

getmetatable.description: Returns the metatable of the object. If the metatable has a __metatable field, returns that value instead.

require.description: Loads the given module. Checks package.loaded first, then searches using package.searchers (5.2+) or package.loaders (5.1). Caches the result.

dofile.description: Opens the named file and executes its contents as a Lua chunk. Returns values returned by the chunk. Without arguments, executes stdin.

loadfile.signature: loadfile([filename [, mode [, env]]]) -> function | nil, string
loadfile.description: Loads a file as a Lua chunk without executing it. Returns the compiled chunk as a function, or nil plus an error message.

load.signature: load(chunk [, chunkname [, mode [, env]]]) -> function | nil, string
load.description: Loads a chunk from a string or function. Returns the compiled chunk as a function, or nil plus an error message.

pcall.description: Calls function f in protected mode. Returns true plus results on success, false plus the error object on failure.

xpcall.description: Like pcall but sets the message handler msgh for error handling. Extra arguments to f supported in Lua 5.2+ and LuaJIT 2.1.

error.description: Raises an error with the given message. Level controls the error position: 1 (default) = where error was called, 2 = caller, 0 = no position.

assert.description: Calls error if v is false or nil; otherwise returns all its arguments. Default message is "assertion failed!".

collectgarbage.description: Generic interface to the garbage collector. Options include "collect", "stop", "restart", "count", "step", "isrunning", "incremental" (5.4), "generational" (5.4).

unpack.description: Returns the elements from the given list. In Lua 5.2+ this is table.unpack. default i is 1, j is #list.

warn.description: Emits a warning with the given message segments. If the first segment starts with "@", it controls warnings: "@on" / "@off". Available in Lua 5.4+.

_G.description: A global variable holding a reference to the global environment table. In Lua 5.2+, just a regular global containing the global table.

_ENV.description: The environment table for the current chunk (Lua 5.2+). Free names are syntactic sugar for _ENV.name. Changing _ENV changes visible globals.

_VERSION.description: A global string containing the running Lua version, e.g. "Lua 5.4".

loadstring.description: Loads a string as a Lua chunk (Lua 5.1). In Lua 5.2+ use load() instead.

module.description: Creates a module (Lua 5.1). Deprecated in Lua 5.2+ in favor of the return-a-table pattern.

string.description: Provides generic string manipulation functions. Strings in Lua are indexed starting at 1, are immutable, and can contain embedded zeros.

format.description: Returns a formatted version of its arguments following the format description, similar to C sprintf. Supports %d, %i, %u, %f, %e, %g, %x, %o, %s, %q, %c, and %%.

find.description: Looks for the first match of pattern in s. Returns start and end indices, and any captures. If plain is true, pattern matching is off.

match.description: Returns the captures from the first match of pattern in s or nil. If no captures, returns the whole match.

gmatch.description: Returns an iterator that produces the successive captures from pattern over s. Lua 5.4 added the optional init parameter.

gsub.description: Returns a copy of s with all (or first n) occurrences of pattern replaced by repl (string, table, or function). Also returns total match count.

sub.description: Returns the substring from position i to j (inclusive). Negative indices count from the end. Default j is -1.

rep.description: Returns n copies of s concatenated, separated by sep (default empty). The sep parameter available since Lua 5.2.

upper.description: Returns a copy of s with all lowercase letters changed to uppercase.

lower.description: Returns a copy of s with all uppercase letters changed to lowercase.

len.description: Returns the length of the string s. Embedded zeros are counted.

byte.description: Returns the internal numeric codes of characters s[i] through s[j]. Defaults: i=1, j=i.

char.description: Returns a string with each character having the numeric code of the corresponding argument.

dump.description: Returns a binary representation of the given function. If strip is true (5.3+), excludes debug info.

pack.description: Returns a binary string containing the values packed according to the format string fmt. Available in Lua 5.3+.

unpack.description: Returns the values packed in string s according to fmt. Also returns the position after the last read byte. Lua 5.3+.

packsize.description: Returns the size of a string resulting from string.pack with the given format, without variable-length options. Lua 5.3+.

table.description: Provides generic functions for table manipulation. Most functions assume the table represents a list/array with integer keys from 1 to n.

insert.description: Inserts value at position pos (default #list+1), shifting subsequent elements up.

remove.description: Removes and returns the element at position pos (default #list), shifting subsequent elements down.

sort.description: Sorts list elements in-place. If comp is given, it must return true when the first element should come before the second.

concat.description: Returns the concatenation of list[i]..sep..list[i+1]..sep..list[j]. Default sep is empty string, i=1, j=#list.

move.description: Copies elements a1[f]..a1[e] into a2[t]..a2[t+e-f]. Default a2 is a1. Available in Lua 5.3+.

pack.description: Returns a new table with all arguments stored into keys 1, 2, etc., and a field 'n' with the total count. Lua 5.2+.

unpack.description: Returns the elements from the given list. Equivalent to return list[i], ..., list[j]. Lua 5.2+ (global unpack in 5.1).

math.description: Provides standard mathematical functions and constants. In Lua 5.3+, functions with annotation 'integer/float' give integer results for integer arguments.

abs.description: Returns the absolute value of x. (integer/float)

ceil.description: Returns the smallest integral value greater than or equal to x.

floor.description: Returns the largest integral value less than or equal to x.

sqrt.description: Returns the square root of x.

sin.description: Returns the sine of x (in radians).

cos.description: Returns the cosine of x (in radians).

tan.description: Returns the tangent of x (in radians).

asin.description: Returns the arc sine of x (in radians).

acos.description: Returns the arc cosine of x (in radians).

atan.description: Returns the arc tangent of y/x (in radians). In Lua 5.3+, replaced math.atan2.

log.description: Returns the logarithm of x in the given base. Default is e (natural log). The base parameter is available Lua 5.2+.

max.description: Returns the argument with the maximum value.

min.description: Returns the argument with the minimum value.

random.description: Returns a pseudo-random number. Without arguments: float in [0,1). With m: integer in [1,m]. With m,n: integer in [m,n]. Lua 5.4 uses xoshiro256**.

randomseed.description: Sets the seed for the pseudo-random generator. In Lua 5.4, called without arguments generates a reasonable seed.

fmod.description: Returns the remainder of x/y that rounds the quotient towards zero.

tointeger.description: If x is representable as a Lua integer, returns it; otherwise returns nil. Lua 5.3+.

type.description: Returns 'integer' if x is an integer, 'float' if a float, or nil if not a number. Lua 5.3+.

ult.description: Returns true if integer m is below integer n when compared as unsigned integers. (5.3+)

io.description: Provides two styles for file manipulation: implicit file handles (io.read, io.write operate on default files) and explicit file handles from io.open.

open.description: Opens a file in the specified mode. Returns a file handle, or nil plus an error. Modes: 'r', 'w', 'a', 'r+', 'w+', 'a+'. Append 'b' for binary.

close.description: Closes the given file handle (or the default output file). Equivalent to file:close().

read.description: Reads from the default input file. Formats: '*n'/'n' (number), '*a'/'a' (whole file), '*l'/'l' (line, default), '*L'/'L' (line with newline).

write.description: Writes each argument to the default output file.

lines.description: Opens the given file and returns an iterator that reads using the given formats. Closes the file when the iterator finishes.

input.description: Sets or gets the default input file. With a filename, opens and sets it. With a file handle, sets it. Without arguments, returns the current default input.

output.description: Sets or gets the default output file.

flush.description: Flushes the default output file. Equivalent to io.output():flush().

tmpfile.description: Returns a handle for a temporary file opened in update mode. Auto-removed on program end.

type.description: Checks whether obj is a valid file handle. Returns "file" for an open handle, "closed file" for a closed handle, or nil otherwise.

popen.description: Execute command and return file handle for pipe. mode: 'r' (read, default) or 'w' (write).

os.description: Provides OS-level functions for date/time, file operations, and process control.

clock.description: Returns an approximation of the CPU time used by the program, in seconds.

time.description: Returns the current time as a number (usually epoch seconds), or the time represented by the given table.

date.description: Returns a string or table with date and time, formatted per the format string. '*t' returns a table.

difftime.description: Returns the difference in seconds between times t2 and t1.

execute.description: Passes command to the OS shell. Returns true/nil, exit type, and exit code (Lua 5.2+). In 5.1 returns a status code.

exit.description: Calls the C exit function. code defaults to true (success). If close is true, closes the Lua state first (5.2+).

getenv.description: Returns the value of the environment variable varname, or nil.

remove.description: Deletes the file with the given name. Returns true on success, or nil plus error message.

rename.description: Renames a file or directory. Returns true on success, or nil plus error message.

tmpname.description: Returns a string with a file name for use as a temporary file.

setlocale.description: Set locale for the program. category: 'all', 'collate', 'ctype', 'monetary', 'numeric', 'time'.

coroutine.description: Provides functions for creating and managing coroutines (cooperative multithreading).

create.description: Creates a new coroutine with body f (a function). Returns the new coroutine (a thread value).

resume.description: Starts or continues execution of coroutine co. Returns true plus yielded/returned values on success, or false plus error.

yield.description: Suspends the running coroutine. Arguments become the extra results of resume.

wrap.description: Creates a coroutine and returns a function that resumes it each time it is called. Raises errors instead of returning false.

status.description: Returns the status of coroutine co: "running", "suspended", "normal", or "dead".

isyieldable.description: Returns true if the running coroutine can yield (not the main thread and not inside a non-yieldable C function). Lua 5.3+.

running.description: Returns the running coroutine plus a boolean: true if the running coroutine is the main thread.

close.description: Closes coroutine co, closing all pending to-be-closed variables and putting the coroutine in a dead state. Lua 5.4+.

debug.description: Provides a debug interface. Functions in this library should be used only for debugging and profiling; use with care in production code.

getinfo.description: Returns a table with information about a function. f can be a function or a stack level. 'what' selects which fields to fill.

traceback.description: Returns a string with a traceback of the call stack. An optional message is prepended.

sethook.description: Sets the given function as a debug hook. Mask is a string with "c" (call), "r" (return), "l" (line).

getlocal.description: Returns the name and value of the local variable at index local of function at level f.

setlocal.description: Assigns value to the local variable at index local of function at stack level.

getupvalue.description: Returns the name and value of the upvalue at index up of function f.

setupvalue.description: Assigns value to the upvalue at index up of function f. Returns the upvalue name or nil.

getmetatable.description: Returns the metatable of value without checking __metatable.

setmetatable.description: Sets the metatable for value to table (can be nil). Returns value.

getuservalue.description: Returns the n-th user value associated with userdata u. Lua 5.4 added the n parameter.

setuservalue.description: Sets value as the n-th user value associated with userdata. Returns udata.

gethook.description: Returns current hook function, mask, and count.

upvalueid.description: Returns unique identifier for upvalue n of function f. (5.2+)

upvaluejoin.description: Makes upvalue n1 of f1 refer to same upvalue as n2 of f2. (5.2+)

package.description: Provides facilities for loading modules. Used by require to find and load modules.

path.description: A string with the path used by require to search for Lua modules. Uses templates with '?' as the module name placeholder.

cpath.description: A string with the path used by require to search for C modules.

loaded.description: A table of already-loaded modules. require checks this table first.

preload.description: A table of loader functions indexed by module name. Checked by require before file searchers.

searchers.description: An array of searcher functions used by require to find a module. In Lua 5.1, this is package.loaders.

searchpath.description: Searches for the given name in the given path. Returns the first file that can be opened, or nil plus an error.

config.description: A string with compile-time configuration for packages (directory separator, path separator, template wildcard, etc.).

loadlib.description: Dynamically links the C library libname and returns funcname as a C function.

utf8.description: Provides basic support for UTF-8 encoding. Available in Lua 5.3+.

char.description: Receives zero or more integers and returns a string with each character having the given code point.

codepoint.description: Returns the code points of all characters in s between positions i and j.

codes.description: Returns an iterator yielding (position, codepoint) for each UTF-8 character in s.

len.description: Returns the number of UTF-8 characters in s between positions i and j. Returns nil plus error position if invalid UTF-8.

offset.description: Returns the byte position where the n-th UTF-8 character starts (counting from position i).

charpattern.description: The pattern "[\0-\x7F\xC2-\xFD][\x80-\xBF]*" which matches exactly one UTF-8 byte sequence.

and.description: Logical AND operator. Returns the first false argument or the last argument. Short-circuit evaluation.

break.description: Terminates the execution of the innermost while, repeat, or for loop.

do.description: Starts a block of code. Paired with 'end'.

else.description: Alternative branch in an if statement.

elseif.description: Additional conditional branch in an if statement. Avoids nesting.

end.description: Closes a block started by function, if, for, while, repeat, or do.

false.description: Boolean false value. Together with nil, is the only falsy value in Lua.

for.description: Starts a for loop, either numeric (for i = a, b, c) or generic (for k, v in iter).

function.description: Declares a function. Can be used as a statement or an expression.

goto.description: Unconditional jump to a label. Available in Lua 5.2+.

in.description: Used in generic for loops: for k, v in iterator do ... end.

local.description: Declares a local variable or function, scoped to the enclosing block.

nil.description: The type nil has one value: nil. Represents the absence of a useful value.

not.description: Logical NOT operator. Returns true if argument is false or nil, otherwise false.

or.description: Logical OR operator. Returns the first true argument or the last argument. Short-circuit evaluation.

repeat.description: Starts a repeat-until loop, which executes at least once.

return.description: Returns values from a function. Must be the last statement in a block.

then.description: Required after the condition in an if or elseif clause.

until.description: Ends a repeat-until loop. The condition is tested after each iteration.

while.description: Starts a while loop that repeats while a condition is true.
